title: hieracium spathophyllum subsp. longiscapum
intro: Hieracium spathophyllum subsp. longiscapum, also known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family Asteraceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and leafy stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation, due to its content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its potential to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in natural remedies. Hawkweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, often used in herbal medicine to address various health concerns.
